  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Roofing nails used in joist hangers instead of specified structural connector nails",
    "Inadequate shear and withdrawal capacity due to incorrect fastener diameter and length",
    "Visible defect cues: large, thin flat heads characteristic of roofing nails"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the installation of the affected joist hangers",
    "Require the subcontractor to remove the roofing nails and replace them with approved structural fasteners per manufacturer specifications",
    "Re-inspect all joist hangers on-site to ensure correct fastener usage before close-out"
  ],
  "rationale": "Roofing nails lack the shear strength and diameter required by Simpson Strong-Tie specifications and building codes (such as the IRC) for structural joist hanger installations. Using them compromises the structural integrity of the floor/roof framing system.",
  "references": [
    "Simpson Strong-Tie Wood Construction Connectors Catalog (Fastener Specifications)",
    "International Residential Code (IRC) Section R301.1.3",
    "International Residential Code (IRC) Table R602.3(1)"
  ]
}
